output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 6/6.
Batch 1: T1 is complete without product-code changes. Verified the existing shot-group single-click selection contract remains `TimelineCanvas -> onSelectClips(group.clipIds) -> TimelineEditor.selectClips`, found no redundant `onShotGroupSelect`-style path, and the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module passed. Wrote the batch checkpoint file.
Batch 2: T2 is complete. The shared selected-clip contract now accepts optional shot metadata on both sides of the boundary: persisted frontend attachments support `shotId`/`shotName`, and backend selected-clip payloads support `shot_id`/`shot_name`. The change is additive-only, so legacy sessions, gallery-only attachments, and older request bodies remain valid. Relevant frontend and edge test modules passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 3: T3, T4, and T5 are complete. Frontend selection now derives shot metadata from `data.config.pinnedShotGroups`, preserves it when timeline and gallery selections are merged, and sends optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields to the agent. Backend normalization, persisted user-turn attachments, and continuation recovery now round-trip optional shot metadata, and timeline loading/summaries now carry explicit shot names through the shared `viewTimeline()` path so both loop-time prompts and runtime `run(command="view")` use the same shot-aware summary. The targeted frontend and edge Vitest modules passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 4: T6 is complete. Same-shot semantics are now centralized in one backend helper, `resolveSelectedClipShot()`, which prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership for selected timeline clips, then generation-history fallback. `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now all use that resolver, `duplicate_generation` keeps the explicit-shot fast path, and prompt text now surfaces structured selected-clip and shared-shot context to the model. The targeted edge Vitest modules passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 5: T7 is complete. Existing edge and frontend suites were extended in place to cover selected-clip shot metadata normalization and continuation recovery, selected-clip prompt and shared-shot system-prompt context, timeline summary shot-group output, same-shot resolver precedence, duplicate fast-path behavior when selected clips already share an explicit `shot_id`, create-task shot reuse, and frontend attachment merge/send payload preservation. The existing `TimelineCanvas` single-click shot-group selection regression remained covered by the unchanged full module, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 6: T8 is complete. The full focused edge and frontend Vitest modules passed, and a temporary shot-context reproduction script was written, run successfully under the repo’s edge Vitest config, and then deleted. That repro verified the end-to-end shot-context path across request normalization, persisted attachment shape, continuation recovery, prompt/system-prompt enrichment, and explicit-shot same-shot resolution.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the existing shot-group selection contract is still the only path: `TimelineCanvas` label single-click calls `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)` and `TimelineEditor` still wires `onSelectClips={selectClips}` with no redundant shot-group selection callback present. Confirmed with the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module.

Extended the shared selected-clip type boundary only: frontend persisted attachments now accept optional `shotId`/`shotName`, and backend selected-clip payloads now accept optional `shot_id`/`shot_name`. The additions are optional, so legacy session turns, gallery-only attachments, and older request bodies remain valid. Verified with the full `useAgentSession.test.tsx` and `selectedClips.test.ts` modules.

Frontend shot metadata now resolves from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` plus `useShots()`, not `resolvedConfig`. `AgentChat` preserves `shotId`/`shotName` when timeline and gallery selections share a URL, and `useSendMessage()` now includes optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields in `selected_clips` without changing legacy attachment behavior. Verified with the full `AgentChat.test.tsx` and `useAgentSession.test.tsx` modules.

Backend selected-clip normalization, persisted user-turn attachments, and continuation recovery now round-trip optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` and `shotId`/`shotName` fields. The changes are additive-only, and prompt enrichment still prefers explicit prompt metadata before DB-derived prompts, so explicit client-supplied shot fields are preserved untouched. Verified with the full `selectedClips.test.ts` and `loop.test.ts` modules.

Timeline state now batch-loads shot names from `shots` for pinned shot ids, threads them through `TimelineState`, `ToolContext`, and the `view_timeline` path, and uses the same shot-aware summary for both loop-time prompt construction and runtime `run(command="view")`. Clip lines now include shot context, and summaries include a `Shot groups:` section with resolved names or `shotId` fallback plus `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`. Verified with the full `loop.test.ts` and `tools/timeline.test.ts` modules.

Added one shared resolver in `tools/clips.ts` that resolves shot context in priority order: explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership for selected timeline clips, then `findShotForGenerations()` fallback. `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now all use that helper, and `duplicate_generation` keeps the fast path because explicit shared `shot_id` returns before any generation-history lookup. Prompt text now surfaces structured selected-clip shot metadata and shared-shot context to the model. Verified with the full `tools/clips.test.ts`, `tools/create-task.test.ts`, and `loop.test.ts` modules.

Extended existing edge and frontend suites only. Added coverage for selected-clip shot metadata normalization, continuation recovery round-trip, selected-clip prompt shot context, shared-shot system prompt guidance, timeline summary shot-group output, same-shot resolver precedence, duplicate fast-path behavior when selected clips already carry a shared explicit `shot_id`, create-task shot reuse, and frontend attachment merge/send payload preservation. The existing `TimelineCanvas` single-click shot-group selection regression remains covered by the unchanged full module. Verified with the full edge and frontend Vitest modules.

Ran the full focused edge and frontend Vitest modules to completion and confirmed they pass with the batch changes applied. Wrote and ran a short temporary reproduction script under the repo's edge Vitest config to verify the shot-context pipeline end to end: request normalization preserved shot metadata, persisted attachments carried shotId/shotName, continuation recovery restored them, prompt/system-prompt text surfaced shared shot context, and same-shot resolution stayed on the explicit-shot fast path without a fallback lookup. The temporary reproduction script was then deleted.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The current UI still uses the original single-click path `TimelineCanvas -> onSelectClips(group.clipIds) -> TimelineEditor.selectClips`, and repository search found no added `onShotGroupSelect`-style callback or second selection path.

Yes. Frontend persisted attachments accept optional `shotId`/`shotName`, backend selected-clip payloads accept optional `shot_id`/`shot_name`, and both changes are additive-only so existing attachment/session shapes still pass unchanged.

Yes. Timeline-selected media now resolves shot membership from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, `AgentChat` preserves timeline shot metadata when deduping against gallery selections, and `useSendMessage()` serializes optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields in `selected_clips`.

Yes. `normalizeSelectedClips()`, `attachSelectedClips()`, and `recoverSelectedClipsFromTurns()` now round-trip optional shot metadata, and prompt enrichment still only backfills prompt text when the client did not already provide it, so explicit shot fields are not clobbered.

Yes. `loadTimelineState()` now loads shot names from `shots`, and the shared `viewTimeline()` summary adds shot annotations to clip lines plus a `Shot groups:` section with resolved shot label or `shotId` fallback, `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`.

Yes. `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now all resolve shot context through the shared `resolveSelectedClipShot()` helper, which prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership, then generation-history fallback.

Yes. The required coverage gaps are closed by extending existing suites only: selected-clip shot metadata normalization and continuation recovery in `selectedClips.test.ts` and `loop.test.ts`, prompt and shared-shot system-prompt context in `loop.test.ts`, timeline summary shot-group output in `tools/timeline.test.ts`, same-shot resolver precedence and duplicate fast path in `tools/clips.test.ts`, create-task shot reuse in `tools/create-task.test.ts`, frontend merge/send payload preservation in `AgentChat.test.tsx` and `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and the existing shot-group single-click selection regression remains covered by the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module run.
Yes. The targeted edge and frontend Vitest runs passed, and the temporary shot-context reproduction script was run successfully under the edge Vitest config and then removed.
